# UniverLab

> UniverLab is an independent computational laboratory that turns real needs into open experiments — developer tools, scientific workflows, and agent systems. Every experiment ships open knowledge: tools, libraries, datasets, and papers. Founded by Jheison Martinez. The site is bilingual (English and Spanish).

UniverLab follows one loop: a real need becomes an open experiment, and that experiment gives back open knowledge — the experiment is the work, and everything it ships is a byproduct. The lab is guided by a philosophy it calls Pensamiento Cósmico: technology as the medium through which curiosity becomes reality, software meant to outlive trends, and knowledge kept open. In practice that means open-source CLIs you can install today, experiments you can reproduce, and documentation that ships with the code.

## Core pages

- [Home](https://univerlab.org/): What UniverLab is, the experiment loop, research directions, and the experiment index.
- [Manifesto — Pensamiento Cósmico](https://univerlab.org/manifesto): The philosophy behind the lab — a philosophy of the continuity of consciousness, and the values that guide which experiments get built.
- [Contributors](https://univerlab.org/contributors): The people and language models behind the lab, and how to contribute.

## Experiments

- [Harness Canopy](https://univerlab.org/canopy): EXP-001 (active) — a CLI-based agentic development ecosystem that gives AI coding agents persistent memory and cross-agent coordination, with RAG, context transfer, mission/sync signalling, and prompt templates.
- [TexForge](https://univerlab.org/texforge): EXP-002 (active) — a unified workspace and CLI for LaTeX that compiles documents to PDF, renders diagrams, and manages templates without installing a multi-gigabyte TeX distribution.
- [GitKit](https://univerlab.org/gitkit): EXP-003 (active) — git tooling that wires up `.gitattributes` and git hooks automatically the moment a repository lands on disk, so every repo starts configured.
- [ghScaff](https://univerlab.org/ghscaff): EXP-004 (active) — scaffolds a complete GitHub repository (manifests, entry points, CI/release workflows) in one step; Rust today, more languages on the way.
- [cadSpec](https://univerlab.org/cadspec): EXP-005 (beta) — declarative CAD as code in TOML: semantic, diffable, reviewable drawings that compile to DXF, built so AI agents can read and generate them.
- [Astro Denoise](https://univerlab.org/astro-denoise): EXP-006 (research) — a reproducible benchmarking platform for denoising methods on astronomical images (a master's thesis project).
- [DemoStage](https://univerlab.org/demostage): EXP-007 (active) — records terminal and app demos as editable event files you can prune, pace, and replay, exporting to cast, html, or gif.
- [Quorum](https://univerlab.org/quorum): EXP-008 (active) — serverless planning poker that runs peer-to-peer over WebRTC: share a link, estimate together, no sign-up required.

## Optional

- [GitHub organization](https://github.com/UniverLab): Source code for every experiment.
- [Spanish site](https://univerlab.org/es): The full site in Spanish.
